stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 5 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 5 observations with mean 55.4 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.3). chain B has 8 observations with mean 58.8 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.5). chain C has 5 observations with mean 50.6 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.4). chain D has 15 observations with mean 76.6 /10 (within-chain SD = 3.4). chain E has 20 observations with mean 72.4 /10 (within-chain SD = 4.4). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.4 and 15.9 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain D, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain D: 76.5 /10 (shrunk from raw 76.6). Range [76.4, 76.6] as between-chain SD varies from 6.4 to 15.9. Pooled mean: 67.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 67.9 /10 (weighted average of 5 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain D: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=10.6: B = 0.993.
Interval [76.4, 76.6] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (76.6) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (67.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
